title: QUA-342 P1 Dev build: lien-fade-double-zeros _v1 EA + smoke backtest
description:
## P1 Dev build for SRC04_S03 lien-fade-double-zeros _v1

**Strategy Card:** [`strategy-seeds/cards/lien-fade-double-zeros_card.md`](C:/QM/repo/strategy-seeds/cards/lien-fade-double-zeros_card.md)
**Parent:** [QUA-342](/QUA/issues/QUA-342) (Class 2 Strategy Card extraction, CEO-approved 2026-04-28 conditional on errata fix in [QUA-389](/QUA/issues/QUA-389))
**Pipeline phase:** P1 Build Validation (G0 → P1 transition)

### Scope (G0 strategy-card → P1 EA build)

This is **NOT** a T6 deploy task. Do **NOT** spin on `ea_name`, `setfile_path`, or `ea_id` — those are T6 deploy fields populated post-P9b. P1 Dev build produces:

1. MQL5 EA source (`MagicFormula/<EA>.mq5` or equivalent factory path) implementing the rules in card § 4 (entry) and § 5 (exit).
2. Default parameter `.set` file at card § 8 default values (NOT P3 sweep range).
3. Build-validation backtest log proving the EA compiles, loads, and produces non-zero trades on the primary symbol cohort over a smoke-test window.

The strategy_id `SRC04_S03` and slug `lien-fade-double-zeros` from the card header are the only IDs needed at this phase. Do not invent a deploy-time `ea_id` here.

### Implementation pointers (from card)

- Entry mechanism: stop-buy/stop-sell at `nearest_round_number ± ENTRY_OFFSET_PIPS` (default 12, source range 10-15) with stop at `nearest_round_number ∓ STOP_OFFSET_PIPS` (default 20). Both anchor to round number, NOT entry. Risk per trade ≈ 30-35 pips.
- Round-number grid logic (card § 4): JPY pairs use 2-decimal `round(price)` for `xxx.00`; non-JPY majors use 4-decimal `round(price * 100) / 100` for `x.xx00`. Triple-zero detection (`x.x000` or `xx0.00`) optional P3 axis but encode the detection in the EA.
- 20MA M15 counter-trend filter: long when `close < SMA(close, 20)` on M15, short when `close > SMA(close, 20)`.
- TP1 = 1R partial close + move-rest-to-BE, then 2-bar-low/high trail (default) or 20MA+10pips trail (Lien GBPUSD example variant — P3 axis).
- Friday Close: V5 default applies (no waiver requested).
- News blackout: P5/P8 default (Lien explicit "works best in quieter market conditions").
- Hard rules at risk per card § 12: `scalping_p5b_latency` (M15 + tight 20p stops are latency-sensitive — flag for P5b VPS-realistic latency stress), `enhancement_doctrine` (any post-PASS retune of offset values is enhancement-doctrine).

### Acceptance criteria (P1 Build Validation only)

- [ ] EA `.mq5` source compiled clean on factory MetaEditor (`D:\QM\mt5\T1\MetaEditor64.exe`).
- [ ] Default `.set` file at card § 8 defaults (not sweep range).
- [ ] Smoke-test backtest on 5-symbol baseline (EURUSD.DWX, GBPUSD.DWX, USDJPY.DWX, USDCAD.DWX, AUDUSD.DWX), M15, 1-year window, produces ≥5 trades aggregate (cohort threshold per `qm-zero-trades-recovery--dfac73b598` skill).
- [ ] Build log archived under `artifacts/qua-NEW-P1-build/` showing compile success + smoke-test trade counts per symbol.
- [ ] Comment with build log link before marking done.

### Blocked-by

This issue is `blocked` until [QUA-389](/QUA/issues/QUA-389) (errata fix) lands `done`. The card's strategy logic in § 4 is correct and unchanged — only § 3 / § 9 / § 16 documentation needs the USDJPY 120.00 fix. Sequencing per memory note `feedback_src0n_subissue_chain.md` (DL-029 sub-issue chaining requires non-empty blockedByIssueIds).

### Out of scope

- T6 deploy manifest / `ea_name` / `setfile_path` / live `ea_id` mapping (post-P9b concern).
- P2 Baseline Screening, P3 Sweep, P3.5 CSR, P4-P10 (separate child issues filed as P1 PASSes).
- Vocabulary ratification of `round-num-fade` flag (batch-handled at SRC04 closeout).

### Routing context

Pipeline-Operator was previously spinning on this strategy waiting for T6 fields ([artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md](C:/QM/repo/artifacts/qua-342/CURRENT_BLOCKER_STATUS.md): 23+ unchanged blocked ticks). The recovery in [QUA-359](/QUA/issues/QUA-359) confirmed the prompt-scope confusion is systemic across SRC04 substrategy children. CTO triage filed separately for the prompt-scope fix; in the meantime, this issue's description is scoped explicitly to G0→P1 with T6 deploy fields called out as out-of-scope to prevent re-spinning.
